spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the feature's general idea but doesn't surface the named spec API symbols directly.

interactive.rationale: Interactive surface exists but is limited — more controls or a richer live readout would help.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Page covers the promises the feature index makes for this concept.

edge_cases.rationale: Happy path is covered but only a few failure modes are surfaced.

references.rationale: Page does not cite the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> reference link — required self-heal anchor.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex, button overrides keep bg+color paired.

openQuestions.title: Surface more named spec API for long-animation-frames-style-duration
openQuestions.detail: The spec summary lists API surfaces this concept doesn't currently name on-page. Either rename the controls / readouts to mention them, or add a second concept page that targets the missing surface directly. The reviewer extracted these from the spec: Long Animation Frames (LoAF) style/layout duration. New PerformanceLongAnimationFrameTiming attributes: styleDuration, layoutDuration. PerformanceScriptTiming gets forcedStyleDuration, forcedLayoutDuration (sync style/layout triggered by script — getComputedStyle, offsetTop). Slot in next to duration/blockingDuration/renderStart/styleAndLayoutStart. Helps distinguish CSS selector cost vs DOM-layou...

openQuestions.title: Cover at least one failure mode
openQuestions.detail: The page currently shows the happy path. Add a UI affordance that drives the feature into a failure mode the spec explicitly calls out (timeout, denied permission, cross-origin block, malformed input) and surface what the browser actually does.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface
openQuestions.detail: Add more controls / a richer live readout so the user can drive the feature through its full input space, not just a single canned example.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must cite chromestatus.com/feature/<id> in its 'see also' or references section — this is the self-heal anchor used by the slug cleanup script.

summary: Concept needs follow-up work before it really lands. 4 open question(s) queued.
